---
title: "Threshold Maps for Infectious Disease Risk"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
setwd("/Users/egepehlivanoglu/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-StockholmUniversity/KVA backup/KVAOneDrive_backup_28Jan2026/2. Projects/4.Cascades/Editorial Review 20250807/map/Anthropocene-maps/6.Infectious_diseases")
source("6.3.histograms.R")
```

# Overview

This report shows maps for the upper tail of the infectious disease risk distribution derived from the Figure 3B workflow.

The maps below retain only the highest values from the histogram distribution:

- top 5%
- top 10%
- top 25%

Assumptions:
+ All 0s and NAs are excluded to calculate the top percentages and excluded from the maps. 

# Top 5%

```{r top5}
threshold_map_plot(5)
```

# Top 10%

```{r top10}
threshold_map_plot(10)
```

# Top 25%

```{r top25}
threshold_map_plot(25)
```
```{r save}
pdf("threshold_maps.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)

plot.new()
text(0.05, 0.92, "Threshold Maps for Infectious Disease Risk", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1.4, font = 2)
text(0.05, 0.80, "Notes and assumptions", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1.1, font = 2)
text(0.05, 0.72, "1. All 0s and NAs are excluded before calculating top-percent thresholds.", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1)
text(0.05, 0.64, "2. The plotted maps also exclude all 0 and NA cells.", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1)
text(0.05, 0.56, "3. First plot = Top 5% of the filtered distribution.", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1)
text(0.05, 0.48, "4. Second plot = Top 10% of the filtered distribution.", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1)
text(0.05, 0.40, "5. Third plot = Top 25% of the filtered distribution.", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1)
text(0.05, 0.28, "All thresholds are based on the Figure 3B infectious disease risk workflow.", adj = c(0, 1), cex = 1)

threshold_map_plot(5)
threshold_map_plot(10)
threshold_map_plot(25)
dev.off()
```
